app_info.subtitle: Use RemNote from ChatGPT
app_info.description: RemNote ChatGPT Bridge lets ChatGPT inspect a running RemNote workspace through a local companion server. It can report live bridge diagnostics and health checks, explain RemNote structure through a built-in capability guide, read focused or selected Rems, inspect ordered children, breadcrumbs, bounded trees, and search results, then create, append, create documents, update, replace, move, reorder, create styled trees, apply structured note batches, or delete focused/selected Rems after local scope and approval checks allow it.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only reports local bridge connection metadata and does not modify RemNote.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not write to public internet state or any external third-party system.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only reports local tool registry, pending request count, and recent request outcomes without modifying RemNote.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads local bridge diagnostics and does not write to public internet state or external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Runs local bridge checks and records pass/fail/skipped results; write execution only occurs when explicitly requested with a sandbox parent Rem.
justifications.open_world_justification: Operates only through the user's private local RemNote bridge and does not publish externally.
justifications.destructive_justification: Never executes destructive delete tools; safe writes require explicit options and plugin-side approval when they affect existing Rems.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only returns a static RemNote capability guide and source URLs.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not write to external systems or publish user data.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not modify, overwrite, move, or delete RemNote content.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only sends a small ping through the local plugin connection and returns the response.
justifications.open_world_justification: Operates only on the local bridge connection and does not publish or send external messages.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not alter RemNote content or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves plugin permission mode and focused Rem availability.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads local plugin state and does not change public or external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or mutate RemNote data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only serializes the Rem currently focused in RemNote.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads local private RemNote data and does not publish it to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not alter, overwrite, or delete RemNote content.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only reads a specific Rem by ID and returns a serialized payload.
justifications.open_world_justification: Accesses the user's local RemNote data without changing public internet state.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not modify or remove the requested Rem.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only reads a bounded Rem subtree with capped depth and serialized output.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads local RemNote content and does not write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not modify, replace, or delete any Rem in the subtree.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only reads one Rem and returns normalized rich text metadata with bounded fields.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads local private RemNote data and does not publish externally.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not modify, replace, move, or delete RemNote content.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only reports focused and selected Rem IDs available from the RemNote editor.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads local editor state and does not write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not alter or remove RemNote data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only reads direct child Rems for one parent and returns capped ordered summaries.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads local RemNote structure without changing public or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not create, update, move, or delete Rems.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only reads the parent chain for one Rem and returns IDs and titles.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads local RemNote hierarchy and does not transmit changes to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not modify or delete any Rem in the breadcrumb chain.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only performs a bounded RemNote SDK search and returns capped result summaries.
justifications.open_world_justification: Searches the user's local RemNote data and does not publish or update external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not create, update, move, overwrite, or delete Rems.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only reads a bounded tree for a requested or focused RemNote document-like root.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads local RemNote structure and does not write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not modify, replace, move, or delete RemNote content.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates a new Rem in the user's RemNote workspace when the plugin permission flow allows it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only the user's private RemNote workspace and does not publish public content.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates new content and does not delete or overwrite existing Rems.

justifications.read_only_justification: Appends a new child Rem under an existing Rem when the plugin permission flow allows it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only the user's private RemNote workspace and does not publish externally.
justifications.destructive_justification: Adds child content without deleting or overwriting the target Rem.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates a document Rem in the user's RemNote workspace when local scope and approval allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only the user's private RemNote workspace and does not publish public content.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates a new document Rem without deleting or overwriting existing Rems.

justifications.read_only_justification: Attempts folder creation only after local policy allows it, then reports SDK_UNSUPPORTED with the installed SDK.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or send external messages; any future folder creation would stay in the private RemNote workspace.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete or overwrite RemNote content and currently performs no mutation because folder creation is unsupported.

justifications.read_only_justification: Updates the visible text of one existing Rem when the plugin permission flow allows it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only the user's private RemNote workspace and does not publish externally.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete Rems or descendants, though it changes existing Rem text after permission.

justifications.read_only_justification: Overwrites the visible text of one existing Rem after local scope and destructive approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only the user's private RemNote workspace and does not publish externally.
justifications.destructive_justification: Overwrites existing Rem text, so it is treated as destructive even though it does not delete children.

justifications.read_only_justification: Moves one Rem under another private RemNote parent when the permission flow allows it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only private RemNote hierarchy and does not write to public internet state.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete or overwrite Rem content; moves with children require approval in the plugin.

justifications.read_only_justification: Reorders direct child Rems under one parent after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only private RemNote hierarchy and does not write to public internet state.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete or overwrite Rem content and rejects child lists that omit or add children.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates a bounded ordered tree of new Rems when the plugin permission flow allows it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Creates content only in the user's private RemNote workspace.
justifications.destructive_justification: Adds new Rems without deleting or overwriting existing RemNote content.

justifications.read_only_justification: Replaces one existing Rem's rich text after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only private RemNote content and does not publish externally.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete Rems, but updates existing Rem text and therefore requires plugin approval.

justifications.read_only_justification: Changes one existing Rem's heading level after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only private RemNote formatting.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete or overwrite note text.

justifications.read_only_justification: Changes one existing Rem's text color after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only private RemNote formatting.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete or overwrite note text; unsupported colors return SDK_UNSUPPORTED.

justifications.read_only_justification: Changes one existing Rem's highlight color after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only private RemNote formatting.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete or overwrite note text; unsupported clearing paths return SDK_UNSUPPORTED.

justifications.read_only_justification: Changes a bounded text range in one existing Rem after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only private RemNote formatting.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete or overwrite note text.

justifications.read_only_justification: Highlights a bounded text range in one existing Rem after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only private RemNote formatting.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete or overwrite note text.

justifications.read_only_justification: Changes one existing Rem's type after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only private RemNote metadata.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete Rems; unsupported normal resets return SDK_UNSUPPORTED.

justifications.read_only_justification: Changes one existing Rem's bullet visibility after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only private RemNote formatting.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete or overwrite note text.

justifications.read_only_justification: Clears formatting on one existing Rem after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only private RemNote formatting.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete Rems; unsupported type-reset cases return SDK_UNSUPPORTED.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates a bounded styled Rem tree under an existing parent after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Creates content only in the user's private RemNote workspace.
justifications.destructive_justification: Adds new Rems without deleting or overwriting existing RemNote content.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates a bounded structured note tree only after local scope and approval checks allow it; dryRun validates without mutation.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only the user's private RemNote workspace and does not publish externally.
justifications.destructive_justification: Adds new Rems without deleting or overwriting existing RemNote content; rollback only removes Rems created by the same failed batch.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates one flashcard Rem under an existing parent after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Creates content only in the user's private RemNote workspace.
justifications.destructive_justification: Adds a new Rem/card without deleting or overwriting existing Rems.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates one concept flashcard Rem under an existing parent after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Creates content only in the user's private RemNote workspace.
justifications.destructive_justification: Adds a new Rem/card without deleting or overwriting existing Rems.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates one descriptor flashcard Rem under an existing parent after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Creates content only in the user's private RemNote workspace.
justifications.destructive_justification: Adds a new Rem/card without deleting or overwriting existing Rems.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates one cloze flashcard Rem under an existing parent after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Creates content only in the user's private RemNote workspace.
justifications.destructive_justification: Adds a new Rem/card without deleting or overwriting existing Rems.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates one multiple-choice card structure under an existing parent after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Creates content only in the user's private RemNote workspace.
justifications.destructive_justification: Adds new Rem/card content without deleting or overwriting existing Rems.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates one list-answer card structure under an existing parent after local scope and approval checks allow it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Creates content only in the user's private RemNote workspace.
justifications.destructive_justification: Adds new Rem/card content without deleting or overwriting existing Rems.

justifications.read_only_justification: Deletes the currently focused Rem only after local scope, preview, typed DELETE, and approval checks pass.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only the user's private RemNote workspace and does not publish externally.
justifications.destructive_justification: Deletes a Rem and may delete descendants when recursive mode is explicitly requested.

justifications.read_only_justification: Deletes exactly one currently selected Rem only after local scope, preview, typed DELETE, and approval checks pass.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only the user's private RemNote workspace and does not publish externally.
justifications.destructive_justification: Deletes a selected Rem and may delete descendants when recursive mode is explicitly requested.

test_cases.description: Check whether the local RemNote bridge is connected.
test_cases.user_prompt: Is my RemNote bridge connected right now?
test_cases.expected_output: Reports whether the local companion server currently has a RemNote plugin connection.

test_cases.description: Inspect live bridge diagnostics when tool visibility looks stale.
test_cases.user_prompt: ChatGPT only shows a few RemNote tools. Check the live bridge diagnostics and tell me the registry count.
test_cases.expected_output: Reports the live tool registry version, tool count, connection status, pending requests, and recent request outcomes.

test_cases.description: Run a safe bridge health check.
test_cases.user_prompt: Run the RemNote bridge health check without writes and tell me which tools passed, failed, or were skipped.
test_cases.expected_output: Records a health-check result with pass/fail/skipped tool rows and avoids destructive delete tools.

test_cases.description: Read the RemNote capability guide before writing notes.
test_cases.user_prompt: Before creating notes, explain Rems, documents, folders, hierarchy, and the best bridge workflow.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the RemNote knowledge pool and recommends structured batch writing for complete notes.

test_cases.description: Verify that ChatGPT can reach the running plugin through the bridge.
test_cases.user_prompt: Ping my RemNote plugin before reading anything.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a successful ping response from the local RemNote plugin connection.

test_cases.description: Inspect plugin permission status before a write.
test_cases.user_prompt: Check my RemNote bridge permission mode and scope before you edit anything.
test_cases.expected_output: Reports the plugin permission mode, active permission scope, approved root if configured, and focused Rem availability.

test_cases.description: Read the Rem currently focused in RemNote.
test_cases.user_prompt: Read the Rem I am focused on in RemNote and summarize what it says.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the focused Rem content in structured form and summarizes it for the user.

test_cases.description: Read a known Rem by ID.
test_cases.user_prompt: Read Rem rem_12345 and tell me the front and back text.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the requested Rem content or a clear not-found error.

test_cases.description: Inspect a small bounded subtree.
test_cases.user_prompt: Inspect Rem rem_12345 and its children up to depth 2.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a bounded serialized Rem tree with truncated markers if limits are reached.

test_cases.description: Read rich Rem metadata when available.
test_cases.user_prompt: Inspect Rem rem_12345 and tell me whether rich text metadata is available.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the Rem content plus rich-support metadata, or a supported=false style result when the installed SDK cannot expose rich fields.

test_cases.description: Read current RemNote selection metadata.
test_cases.user_prompt: Tell me which Rem is focused and which Rems are selected in RemNote.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns focused and selected Rem IDs when the SDK exposes selection data, or a supported=false result with a clear reason.

test_cases.description: Create a new Rem from markdown after permission flow allows it.
test_cases.user_prompt: Create a new Rem under rem_parent_1 with this markdown: ## Study plan\n- Review proofs\n- Practice recall.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates the Rem only after the plugin permission flow allows it and returns the new Rem ID.

test_cases.description: Append a child Rem after user approval.
test_cases.user_prompt: Append a child under my focused Rem with three follow-up questions.
test_cases.expected_output: Reads the focused Rem, proposes child markdown, waits for approval when required, and returns the appended child Rem ID.

test_cases.description: Inspect direct children in RemNote order.
test_cases.user_prompt: Show the direct children under Rem rem_parent_1 in their current order.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns capped child summaries with zero-based indexes in RemNote order.

test_cases.description: Read breadcrumb context before editing.
test_cases.user_prompt: Before changing Rem rem_child_1, show me where it lives in my RemNote hierarchy.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the Rem parent chain with Rem IDs and titles so the user can confirm context.

test_cases.description: Search Rems with bounded results.
test_cases.user_prompt: Find my Rems about spaced repetition, but only show the top 10 matches.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns capped matching Rem summaries and indicates whether results were truncated.

test_cases.description: Inspect current document-like tree.
test_cases.user_prompt: Show a bounded tree for the document I am currently viewing in RemNote.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a bounded tree rooted at the focused portal or focused Rem with truncation markers.

test_cases.description: Update one Rem after permission flow allows it.
test_cases.user_prompt: Update Rem rem_12345 to say: Updated definition for active recall.
test_cases.expected_output: Updates only the target Rem text after the plugin permission flow allows it.

test_cases.description: Move one Rem after approval.
test_cases.user_prompt: Move Rem rem_child_1 under rem_parent_2 at index 0.
test_cases.expected_output: Moves only the target Rem to the requested parent and index after local scope and approval checks allow it.

test_cases.description: Create an ordered Rem tree.
test_cases.user_prompt: Create a three-item outline under rem_parent_1 with children First, Second, and Third in that exact order.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates a bounded tree after permission flow allows it and preserves the provided child order.

test_cases.description: Apply a structured styled note batch.
test_cases.user_prompt: Under rem_parent_1, create one styled study note with a heading, one child bullet, and the inline equation $E=mc^2$.
test_cases.expected_output: Validates the structured batch, waits for approval when required, writes the complete styled note tree, and returns created Rem IDs plus verification details when requested.

test_cases.description: Create a RemNote document.
test_cases.user_prompt: Create a new RemNote document under rem_parent_1 titled Weekly Review.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates a document Rem after local scope and approval checks allow it, then returns the new Rem ID.

test_cases.description: Report unsupported folder creation honestly.
test_cases.user_prompt: Create a RemNote folder called Research Inbox under rem_parent_1.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a clear SDK_UNSUPPORTED error if the installed RemNote SDK does not expose folder creation.

test_cases.description: Replace one Rem after destructive approval.
test_cases.user_prompt: Replace the text of Rem rem_12345 with: Final definition of active recall.
test_cases.expected_output: Shows destructive approval in RemNote, then replaces only the target Rem text if approved.

test_cases.description: Reorder direct children deterministically.
test_cases.user_prompt: Reorder the children of rem_parent_1 to this exact order: rem_c, rem_a, rem_b.
test_cases.expected_output: Reorders only if the provided list exactly matches the current direct child IDs.

test_cases.description: Delete the focused Rem with strict confirmation.
test_cases.user_prompt: Delete the Rem I am focused on in RemNote. I confirm with DELETE.
test_cases.expected_output: Shows a delete preview in RemNote and deletes only after the user types DELETE and approves.

test_cases.description: Delete exactly one selected Rem with strict confirmation.
test_cases.user_prompt: Delete the selected Rem in RemNote. I confirm with DELETE.
test_cases.expected_output: Deletes exactly one selected Rem only after preview, typed DELETE, and user approval.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for unrelated calendar requests.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: What meetings do I have tomorrow morning?
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because it has no calendar workflow.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not expose direct OpenAI configuration inside RemNote.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Set my OpenAI API key in the RemNote plugin and switch to a different model.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should explain that the plugin does not store OpenAI API keys or choose models.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not delete without strict confirmation.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Delete this Rem from RemNote without asking me again.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not call a delete tool unless the request targets focused or selected Rem and includes the required DELETE confirmation path.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not attempt unbounded knowledge-base extraction.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Export my entire RemNote knowledge base into this chat.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should decline unbounded extraction and offer focused or bounded Rem reads instead.
